ksa_text: Skill in writing shell scripts to automate data operations, including file manipulation, job scheduling, environment configuration, log processing, and pipeline orchestration on Linux systems.

dcwf.top_element_label: Knowledge of basic system administration, network, and operating system hardening techniques.

nist_ai_rmf.top_element_label: Processes are in place for robust engagement with relevant AI actors.

top_anchors.element_label: Knowledge of basic system administration, network, and operating system hardening techniques.

pass_2_evaluation.rationale: Technical Foundations falls outside the primary competency scope of the Data and AI Transformation Lead. This skill KSA does not align with the role's core responsibilities and is excluded.

pass_3_adversarial_internal.ruling: No internal inconsistency identified. Domain scope, seniority fit, and signal level are internally consistent with exclusion.

pass_4_adversarial_external.challenge: Is Technical Foundations truly out-of-scope for the Data and AI Transformation Lead? Emerging role definitions may include broader competency expectations.
pass_4_adversarial_external.evidence: Current job market analysis and framework definitions (DCWF, DDAT, NICE) do not list Technical Foundations as a core or recommended competency for the Data and AI Transformation Lead. Role scope exclusion is consistent with external evidence.
pass_4_adversarial_external.ruling: Upheld: external evidence confirms Technical Foundations falls outside the established competency profile for the Data and AI Transformation Lead. Exclusion maintained.

decision_rationale: Technical Foundations is outside the competency scope of the Data and AI Transformation Lead. All evaluation passes consistently support exclusion.
